plugins.name: Cross-Volume Write Fix (0.1.2)
plugins.author: 6ix
plugins.description: Fixes a Paralives 0.1.2 regression that breaks Workshop mod loading (and some save writes) when your Steam library is on a different drive than Windows %TEMP%. The devs' new atomic-write helper uses Path.GetTempFileName() (under %TEMP%) + File.Replace, which throws Win32 error 1176 (ERROR_UNABLE_TO_MOVE_REPLACEMENT) cross-volume. This patch routes the temp file to the same directory as the destination so File.Replace works. Install if you see 'IOException: Win32 IO returned 1176' in your BepInEx log, or if Workshop mods stopped loading after 0.1.2.

plugins.name: Plugin Hub
plugins.author: 6ix
plugins.description: Browse, install, and toggle Paralives mods from inside the game. Auto-updates itself and your installed mods on each launch — no manual file copying after the first install. Also fixes the Steam-offline boot hang so the game launches without internet.

plugins.name: Stats Overlay
plugins.author: 6ix
plugins.description: Unity-Editor-style stats panel — FPS, CPU/render times, draw calls, triangles, audio level, animation counts. Top-left overlay. Press F3 to toggle.

plugins.name: Menu FPS Limiter
plugins.author: 6ix
plugins.description: Caps the frame rate at 60 in the main menu and 30 when the window isn't focused (alt-tab). Gameplay runs uncapped by default. Stops your fans screaming while the menu sits at 200+ fps doing nothing. All three thresholds configurable.

plugins.name: Not Enough Workshop Mods
plugins.author: 6ix
plugins.description: Fixes the Steam Workshop 50-mod cap. Without it, any subscribed mods past #50 get DELETED from your computer on game launch. Default safety cap is 15,000 mods — raise it in the cfg if you somehow have more. Also loads your downloaded Workshop mods when Steam is offline (vanilla would skip them). Install if you use Workshop.
